strategy_outcomes.name: ETH 1h Value Recovery Scale-In v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: price dislocates away from fast value, reclaims it, and the position scales in only when the reclaim improves on the next bar
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: ETH 1h is the primary lane and sits in a chop-to-transition mix. That should produce repeated failed extensions and value recoveries, but the all-in first reclaim is fragile. A scale-in only after the reclaim proves itself gives denser participation without the ceremonial sparsity that killed earlier cycles.

long: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 below initial entry
Exit remaining 25% on touch of EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 above initial entry
Exit remaining 25% on touch of EMA_55 or 5 bars

strategy_outcomes.rationale: 0 trades on valid data (integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples; integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples) — Quandalf must explicitly choose iterate or abort.

strategy_outcomes.name: BTC 1h Asymmetric Continuation v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: a continuation trigger is taken only after directional pressure reasserts above fast structure, while stop logic and exit logic are intentionally separated to create asymmetry
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: The cycle explicitly asks for continuation with asymmetric exit logic where trigger, risk, and exit components play different roles. BTC 1h is the best-fit lane because it carries more directional transition quality than ETH 1h while staying liquid enough to test whether separated exit logic improves payoff asymmetry.

long: Stop loss: 0.88 * ATR_14 below entry or below EMA_55, whichever is tighter
short: Stop loss: 0.88 * ATR_14 above entry or above EMA_55, whichever is tighter

strategy_outcomes.rationale: 0 trades on valid data (integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples; integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples) — Quandalf must explicitly choose iterate or abort.

strategy_outcomes.name: AXS 1h Retest Hold Timeout v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: a break from local structure is not chased; price must retest and hold the broken level, then the trade is de-risked by time if expansion does not appear fast enough
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: The cycle explicitly asks for retest-and-hold continuation with time-based failure control. AXS 1h is the best structurally different lane for this because it is expressive enough for visible transitions and should produce denser retest events than the 4h lanes that kept failing. The edge is the accepted retest, not the first break.

long: De-risk to break-even if no 0.28 * ATR_14 favorable move within 2 bars
short: De-risk to break-even if no 0.28 * ATR_14 favorable move within 2 bars

guidance.instruction: Work strategy-by-strategy and queue-row-by-queue-row. For each spec, choose exactly one overall decision: pass, refine, or abort. Inside that strategy decision, include one queue_decision for every queue row in the packet. If a strategy produced 0 trades on valid data, treat that as a red flag that must resolve explicitly as either refine or abort. Do not omit any tested row and do not use promote, fix_only, skip, pending, or undecided.
